title: PTEROSTILBENE
description: Pterostilbene is a naturally occurring polyphenolic compound, primarily found in blueberries and grapes, that is a dimethylated analog of resveratrol with superior bioavailability. It functions as a potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory agent, and sirtuin activator, with effects on cellular metabolism, epigenetic regulation, and NAD+ pathways. Research suggests applications in metabolic health, cognitive function, longevity, and cancer prevention.

research.title: Dietary Supplementation With NAD+-Boosting Compounds in Humans: Current Knowledge and Future Directions
research.summary: Review examining NAD+ precursor supplementation including nicotinamide riboside co-administered with pterostilbene, finding these compounds are common candidates for improving physiologic function in aging and age-related diseases.

research.title: Pterostilbene Targets Hallmarks of Aging in the Gene Expression Landscape in Blood of Healthy Rats
research.summary: Pterostilbene supplementation in healthy rats modulated gene expression related to key aging hallmarks, suggesting anti-aging effects at the molecular level through epigenetic mechanisms.

research.title: A randomized placebo-controlled trial of nicotinamide riboside and pterostilbene supplementation in experimental muscle injury in elderly individuals
research.summary: Clinical trial examining NR and pterostilbene supplementation effects on muscle regeneration and stem cell function in elderly individuals following muscle injury.

research.title: Pterostilbene, a dimethylated analog of resveratrol, promotes energy metabolism in obese rats
research.summary: Study found pterostilbene significantly suppressed abdominal fat accumulation, increased oxygen consumption and energy expenditure, and promoted fat metabolism over carbohydrate metabolism in obese rats.

research.title: Pterostilbene-induced changes in gut microbiota composition in relation to obesity
research.summary: Pterostilbene supplementation induced beneficial changes in gut microbiota composition associated with improvements in obesity-related metabolic parameters.

research.title: AMPK-Dependent Epigenetic Regulation of Metabolism Mediates the Anti-Cancer Action of Pterostilbene in Hepatocellular Carcinoma
research.summary: Pterostilbene demonstrated anti-cancer effects in hepatocellular carcinoma through AMPK-dependent epigenetic regulation of metabolism, suggesting potential therapeutic applications.

research.title: Dietary Pterostilbene for MTA1-Targeted Interception in High-Risk Premalignant Prostate Cancer
research.summary: Dietary pterostilbene showed promise for cancer prevention in high-risk premalignant prostate cancer through targeted molecular mechanisms, supporting its potential for nutritional intervention.

research.title: Pterostilbene enhances reproductive outcomes and oocyte quality in aged mice without adverse effects
research.summary: Long-term pterostilbene supplementation improved reproductive outcomes, increased implantation and live birth rates in aged mice, and enhanced oocyte quality without affecting estrous cycles or offspring health.

sentiment.summary: Users report pterostilbene as an effective longevity and anti-aging supplement, particularly when combined with NAD+ precursors like NR. Most users taking it as part of comprehensive stacks report positive results for energy, cognitive function, and overall health markers, though it's rarely used alone.

effects.label: Synergistic Effects:
effects.description: Most effective when combined with NAD+ boosters like NR or NMN, with users reporting the combination works better than either alone

effects.label: Bioavailability Advantage:
effects.description: Users choose pterostilbene over resveratrol due to its superior absorption and lipophilicity, noting it's more cost-effective despite higher price

effects.label: Long-term Benefits:
effects.description: Users report sustained benefits over months to years, particularly for anti-aging goals, with some showing measurable improvements in biomarkers

effects.label: Individual Variation:
effects.description: Effectiveness varies significantly between users, with some reporting dramatic results while others notice minimal changes

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: Most commonly used at 50-150mg daily, often as part of combination products with NR

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Typically taken in the morning on empty stomach or with breakfast as part of longevity stacks

dosage.label: Cycling:
dosage.description: Some users cycle pterostilbene as part of senolytic protocols rather than taking daily

dosage.label: Stack Integration:
dosage.description: Usually combined with 250-300mg NR, quercetin, fisetin, and other longevity compounds

sideEffects.label: Minimal Reported:
sideEffects.description: Very few direct side effects attributed to pterostilbene specifically in user reports

sideEffects.label: Stack Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Users occasionally report difficulty determining which compound in complex stacks causes effects or side effects

sideEffects.label: Cost Concerns:
sideEffects.description: High cost is the main complaint, with users seeking more affordable alternatives or questioning cost-effectiveness

sideEffects.label: Limited Standalone Data:
sideEffects.description: Most users take it in combination, making it difficult to isolate specific side effects

availability.label: Commercial Products:
availability.description: Available from supplement companies, often combined with NR in branded products like Basis and Tru Niagen

availability.label: Quality Brands:
availability.description: Users prefer brands that provide third-party testing for purity and potency verification

availability.label: Food Sources:
availability.description: Naturally found in blueberries, though supplemental doses are much higher than dietary intake
